title: Kaleid cross-source spot check on committed video fixture

metricComparisons.description: higher kaleid fold counts should materially reorganize decoded-media structure into a tighter symmetric pattern
higher kaleid fold counts should materially raise rendered decoded-media energy, not leave the audio static

expectedVideo: the decoded fixture should gain stronger radial symmetry as nSides rises
higher fold counts should stay decodable and intentional rather than producing broken seams
expectedAudio: fixture audio should pick up the same increased fold complexity heard on the osc case
the rendered mix should stay stable and free of runaway clipping
manualChecks: Does kaleid remain aesthetically usable on decoded footage, not just procedural osc?
Does the stronger setting still feel like one coherent operator across sight and sound?
